articles.headline: India Unveils 'National AI & Data Governance Framework 2025' to Drive Inclusive Digital Transformation
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved a comprehensive framework to guide the development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and manage data governance across sectors.
• The framework emphasizes ethical AI principles, data privacy, security, and the responsible use of AI for public good, aligning with India's digital public infrastructure (DPI) vision.
• Key components include establishing an 'AI Ethics Council', a 'National Data Protection Authority', and guidelines for anonymization and secure data sharing.
• The policy aims to foster innovation while mitigating risks associated with AI, ensuring equitable access and benefits for all citizens.
• It is seen as a crucial step towards making India a global leader in responsible AI and digital economy.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential
Role of IT in governance
prelimsPoints: The framework is named 'National AI & Data Governance Framework 2025'.
It was approved by the Union Cabinet.
Key bodies to be established: AI Ethics Council, National Data Protection Authority.
Focus areas: ethical AI, data privacy, security, responsible AI use.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of a national AI and data governance framework for India's digital future.
Analyze the challenges in implementing ethical AI and data privacy regulations in a diverse country like India.
Evaluate how the framework can leverage India's Digital Public Infrastructure for inclusive growth and innovation.
Examine the role of the proposed AI Ethics Council and National Data Protection Authority in ensuring responsible AI development.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration depicting digital infrastructure and AI symbols converging over a map of India.
articles.trivia: India's digital public infrastructure, including UPI and Aadhaar, is often cited as a global model for inclusive digital transformation.

articles.headline: Veteran Karnataka Politician R. V. Devraj Passes Away at 67
articles.explainer:
• R. V. Devraj, a prominent figure in Karnataka politics and former MLA, passed away at the age of 67.
• He served as a Member of the Legislative Assembly (MLA) for Karnataka from 2004 to 2008.
• Devraj also held the distinguished position of Chairman of the Karnataka State Road Transport Corporation (KSRTC) from 2000 to 2007.
• He was known for his contributions to public service and transport sector reforms in the state.
• His demise marks the loss of an experienced leader in state politics.
gsSubTopics: Important personalities
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
prelimsPoints: R. V. Devraj was a former MLA from Karnataka (2004-2008).
He served as Chairman of KSRTC (2000-2007).
His age at passing was 67.
articles.imageDescription: Black and white portrait of R. V. Devraj.
articles.trivia: State Road Transport Corporations (SRTCs) play a vital role in providing affordable public transport, especially in rural areas, and are often headed by political appointees.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% Amidst Global Headwinds, Focus on Inflation Management
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach amidst persistent global economic uncertainties and domestic inflationary pressures, particularly from food prices.
• RBI Governor highlighted the need for continued vigilance to bring inflation within the target band of 2-6% sustainably.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure that inflation progressively aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts suggest this stance indicates a preference for stability and a wait-and-watch approach before considering any rate cuts.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Current repo rate: 6.5%.
This is the sixth consecutive time the rate has been unchanged.
RBI's inflation target band: 2-6%.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is responsible for setting the repo rate.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate in the current economic scenario.
Discuss the impact of sustained high-interest rates on India's economic growth and investment climate.
Examine the challenges faced by the RBI in managing inflation, particularly the role of supply-side factors like food prices.
Evaluate the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance and its implications for future monetary policy decisions.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Enter Crucial Final Phase, Focus on Market Access and IPR
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for the ambitious India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have entered their final and most crucial phase.
• Key sticking points include greater market access for agricultural products, automotive, and services, alongside robust provisions for Intellectual Property Rights (IPR) protection.
• Both sides express optimism for a breakthrough by mid-2026, aiming to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment.
• The FTA is expected to provide Indian industries with preferential access to the large EU market and attract European investment into India.
• Successful conclusion would mark a strategic geopolitical and economic partnership between two of the world's largest economies.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India-EU FTA negotiations are in their final phase.
Key areas of discussion: market access (agriculture, automotive, services) and IPR.
Expected completion by mid-2026.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic and economic significance of a comprehensive Free Trade Agreement between India and the European Union.
Analyze the major challenges and opportunities for India in negotiating market access and IPR provisions with the EU.
Evaluate the potential impact of the India-EU FTA on India's domestic industries and overall economic growth.
Examine how this FTA could reshape global trade dynamics and India's position in multilateral trade forums.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union displayed together.
articles.trivia: The EU is India's third-largest trading partner, and India is the EU's 10th largest trading partner.

articles.headline: New 'National Wetlands Conservation and Management Program 2025' Launched for Holistic Protection
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched an ambitious 'National Wetlands Conservation and Management Program 2025'.
• The program aims for holistic protection, sustainable use, and restoration of India's diverse wetland ecosystems.
• It integrates community participation, scientific research, and capacity building for effective wetland management at local levels.
• Emphasis is placed on inventorying and mapping all wetlands, categorizing them based on ecological significance, and developing site-specific management plans.
• This initiative underscores India's commitment to Ramsar Convention principles and biodiversity conservation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and Climate Change.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Program name: 'National Wetlands Conservation and Management Program 2025'.
Launched by: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
Key aspects: holistic protection, sustainable use, community participation, scientific research, capacity building.
Aligned with: Ramsar Convention.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and economic significance of wetlands in India and the threats they face.
Analyze the key features of the 'National Wetlands Conservation and Management Program 2025' and its potential effectiveness.
Evaluate the role of community participation and scientific research in the successful implementation of wetland conservation initiatives.
Examine how this program aligns with India's international commitments under the Ramsar Convention and broader biodiversity goals.
articles.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with migratory birds and lush vegetation.
articles.trivia: India has over 75 Ramsar Sites, covering a large ecological area, designated as Wetlands of International Importance.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds 'Right to Disconnect' Legislation, Citing Employee Well-being and Privacy
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India delivered a landmark judgment, upholding the constitutional validity of the 'Right to Disconnect' legislation.
• The law, enacted by several states, grants employees the right to not respond to work-related communications outside of designated working hours.
• The Court emphasized the importance of work-life balance, mental health, and the right to privacy for employees in the digital age.
• It clarified that while employers have a right to reasonable expectation of availability, it cannot infringe upon an individual's personal time and mental well-being.
• This ruling is expected to influence national labor laws and corporate policies, promoting healthier workplace cultures.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: The Supreme Court upheld the 'Right to Disconnect' legislation.
It cited employee well-being and privacy as key reasons.
The legislation allows employees to ignore work communications outside work hours.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional basis for the 'Right to Disconnect' in India, linking it to fundamental rights like the right to life and privacy.
Analyze the socio-economic implications of this judgment for both employees and employers in the Indian context.
Examine how the 'Right to Disconnect' contributes to employee mental health and work-life balance in the increasingly digitalized workplace.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in implementing such a right across diverse sectors and industries in India.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on law books, symbolizing a court judgment.
articles.trivia: France was one of the first countries to introduce a 'right to disconnect' law in 2017.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches 'Shukrayaan-2' Mission to Venus, Expanding India's Interplanetary Footprint
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved another milestone with the successful launch of 'Shukrayaan-2', India's second mission to Venus.
• The mission aims to study the Venusian atmosphere, surface, and subsurface composition, focusing on its geological and atmospheric evolution.
• Shukrayaan-2 carries a suite of advanced scientific payloads developed by Indian and international research institutions.
• This mission reinforces India's growing capabilities in deep-space exploration and planetary science.
• Data from Shukrayaan-2 is expected to provide crucial insights into Venus's extreme environment and potential for past habitability.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: Mission name: 'Shukrayaan-2'.
Target planet: Venus.
Launching agency: ISRO.
Primary objective: study Venusian atmosphere, surface, and subsurface.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's 'Shukrayaan-2' mission in advancing planetary science and space exploration.
Analyze the scientific objectives of studying Venus and how they contribute to our understanding of planetary evolution.
Evaluate the role of international collaboration in ISRO's deep-space missions and its implications for global scientific cooperation.
Examine the challenges involved in designing and executing missions to planets with extreme environments like Venus.
articles.imageDescription: Artist's impression of the Shukrayaan-2 orbiter approaching Venus.
articles.trivia: Venus is often called Earth's 'sister planet' due to its similar size and mass, but its extremely hot and dense atmosphere makes it vastly different.

articles.headline: India-Nepal Joint Military Exercise 'Surya Kiran XV' Concludes, Enhancing Bilateral Security Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The 15th edition of the joint military exercise 'Surya Kiran' between India and Nepal concluded successfully in Uttarakhand.
• The exercise focused on enhancing interoperability in counter-terrorism operations, disaster relief, and humanitarian assistance in mountainous terrain.
• Troops from both armies shared best practices and tactical skills through simulated scenarios and joint drills.
• 'Surya Kiran' is a significant annual event that strengthens defense cooperation and fosters mutual understanding between the two nations.
• The exercise underscored the deep-rooted strategic partnership and friendly ties between India and Nepal.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: Exercise name: 'Surya Kiran XV'.
Participating countries: India and Nepal.
Focus: counter-terrorism, disaster relief, humanitarian assistance in mountainous terrain.
Location for this edition: Uttarakhand.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of joint military exercises like 'Surya Kiran' for India's border security and regional stability.
Analyze how such exercises contribute to enhancing interoperability and mutual trust between the armed forces of India and Nepal.
Evaluate the evolving nature of India-Nepal defense cooperation in the context of changing geopolitical dynamics in the Himalayan region.
Examine the role of military diplomacy in strengthening bilateral relations between neighboring countries.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and Nepali soldiers conducting a joint drill in mountainous terrain.
articles.trivia: The 'Surya Kiran' exercise is held alternately in India and Nepal.

articles.headline: Government Announces New PLI Scheme for Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing, Boosting 'Make in India'
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government unveiled a new Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme specifically tailored for advanced semiconductor manufacturing and design.
• This scheme aims to attract global semiconductor majors to set up fabrication units and design centers in India.
• It offers substantial financial incentives, tax breaks, and infrastructure support to foster a robust domestic semiconductor ecosystem.
• The initiative is critical for India's strategic autonomy in electronics manufacturing and reducing dependence on imports.
• It aligns with the 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' visions, positioning India as a global hub for semiconductor production.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
articles.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsPoints: New PLI scheme targets advanced semiconductor manufacturing and design.
Objective: attract global majors, foster domestic ecosystem.
Part of 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives.
Offers financial incentives, tax breaks, infrastructure support.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing a robust domestic semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem for India's economic and national security.
Analyze the key features of the new PLI scheme for semiconductors and its potential to attract foreign direct investment (FDI) and boost local production.
Evaluate the challenges India faces in becoming a global hub for semiconductor manufacturing, including infrastructure, skilled labor, and technological expertise.
Examine how this initiative aligns with India's broader goals of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' and digital sovereignty.
articles.imageDescription: A microchip with interconnected circuits, symbolizing semiconductor technology.
articles.trivia: The global semiconductor industry is highly concentrated, with Taiwan and South Korea dominating advanced chip manufacturing.

articles.headline: National Health Mission 2.0 Rolled Out, Prioritizing Preventive Healthcare and Digital Health Records
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare launched 'National Health Mission 2.0' (NHM 2.0), a revamped version of the flagship health program.
• NHM 2.0 places strong emphasis on preventive and promotive healthcare, alongside strengthening primary and secondary health infrastructure.
• A key feature is the nationwide implementation of digital health records and telehealth services under the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM).
• The mission aims to address health disparities, improve maternal and child health outcomes, and combat non-communicable diseases (NCDs) more effectively.
• It seeks to achieve universal access to quality healthcare services, aligning with the vision of 'Health for All'.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
articles.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: Program name: 'National Health Mission 2.0'.
Launched by: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare.
Key focus areas: preventive healthcare, digital health records, strengthening primary/secondary health.
Integrates with: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM).
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of India's public health policy from NHM to NHM 2.0, highlighting the shifts in priorities and strategies.
Analyze the potential impact of digital health records and telehealth services under NHM 2.0 on healthcare accessibility and efficiency in rural areas.
Evaluate the challenges in achieving universal health coverage and addressing health disparities through the new mission.
Examine the importance of preventive and promotive healthcare in reducing the burden of non-communicable diseases in India.
articles.imageDescription: A doctor interacting with a patient using a tablet, symbolizing digital healthcare.
articles.trivia: The original National Health Mission (NHM) was launched in 2013 by subsuming the National Rural Health Mission (NRHM) and the National Urban Health Mission (NUHM).

articles.headline: India Reiterates Call for Comprehensive UNSC Reforms at UN General Assembly Session
articles.explainer:
• India's Permanent Representative to the UN strongly advocated for comprehensive reforms of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) during the ongoing UN General Assembly session.
• India emphasized the need for an expanded and more representative UNSC that reflects contemporary global realities, including increasing the number of permanent and non-permanent members.
• The call highlighted the inadequacy of the current structure, which dates back to 1945, in addressing 21st-century challenges effectively.
• India, along with other G4 nations (Brazil, Germany, Japan), has consistently pushed for a permanent seat on the Security Council.
• Reforms are deemed crucial for enhancing the legitimacy, effectiveness, and credibility of the UN's principal organ responsible for international peace and security.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India advocates for UNSC reforms.
Key demand: expansion of permanent and non-permanent members.
India is part of the G4 nations (Brazil, Germany, Japan, India) pushing for permanent seats.
UNSC structure dates back to 1945.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's long-standing demand for a permanent seat in the UNSC and the justifications for its inclusion.
Analyze the arguments for and against the expansion of the UNSC, considering the interests of various member states.
Evaluate the implications of the current UNSC structure on its effectiveness and legitimacy in addressing global conflicts and crises.
Examine the role of the G4 nations in advocating for UNSC reforms and the challenges they face in achieving consensus.
articles.imageDescription: The United Nations General Assembly hall in New York.
articles.trivia: The UN Security Council has 15 members: five permanent members with veto power (China, France, Russia, United Kingdom, United States) and ten non-permanent members elected for two-year terms.

articles.headline: New Report Highlights Severe Air Pollution Levels in Major Indian Cities, Calls for Stricter NCAP Implementation
articles.explainer:
• A recent report by a leading environmental watchdog revealed alarming levels of air pollution in several major Indian cities, consistently exceeding safe limits.
• The report attributed the pollution to a combination of vehicular emissions, industrial activities, construction dust, and stubble burning in neighboring regions.
• It urged for the stringent and expedited implementation of the National Clean Air Program (NCAP) and greater inter-state cooperation.
• The findings underscore severe public health implications, including respiratory diseases and reduced life expectancy.
• Experts called for a multi-sectoral approach involving policy changes, technological solutions, and public awareness campaigns to tackle the crisis.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Report highlights severe air pollution in major Indian cities.
Causes: vehicular emissions, industrial activity, construction dust, stubble burning.
Calls for stricter implementation of National Clean Air Program (NCAP).
Impact: public health issues, respiratory diseases.
mainsPoints: Discuss the major sources and impacts of air pollution in Indian cities, particularly during the winter months.
Critically evaluate the effectiveness of the National Clean Air Program (NCAP) and suggest measures for its stronger implementation.
Analyze the role of inter-state cooperation and technological solutions in mitigating air pollution, especially issues like stubble burning.
Examine the public health burden caused by air pollution and the need for a multi-sectoral approach to address this environmental crisis.
articles.imageDescription: A hazy cityscape with a silhouette of buildings under a polluted sky.
articles.trivia: The World Health Organization (WHO) air quality guidelines recommend an annual average PM2.5 concentration of no more than 5 µg/m³ (micrograms per cubic meter).

articles.headline: Lok Sabha Passes 'Electoral Reforms Bill 2025', Aiming to Streamline Voter Registration and Campaign Finance
articles.explainer:
• The Lok Sabha passed the 'Electoral Reforms Bill 2025' after extensive debate, which now moves to the Rajya Sabha for consideration.
• The Bill introduces significant changes aimed at streamlining voter registration by linking it with Aadhaar, ensuring transparent campaign finance, and curbing electoral malpractices.
• Provisions include enhanced powers for the Election Commission of India (ECI) to enforce the Model Code of Conduct and regulate anonymous donations.
• Opposition parties raised concerns regarding potential privacy infringements with Aadhaar linkage and the impact on electoral transparency.
• The government asserts the Bill will strengthen democratic processes and make elections more fair and inclusive.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: Bill name: 'Electoral Reforms Bill 2025'.
Passed by: Lok Sabha, moves to Rajya Sabha.
Key features: Aadhaar linkage for voter registration, transparent campaign finance, enhanced ECI powers.
Concerns raised: privacy, electoral transparency.
mainsPoints: Discuss the need for electoral reforms in India, particularly concerning voter registration and campaign finance.
Analyze the proposed changes in the 'Electoral Reforms Bill 2025', focusing on the implications of Aadhaar linkage and ECI's enhanced powers.
Evaluate the arguments for and against the Bill, particularly concerning privacy concerns and the balance between transparency and individual rights.
Examine how these reforms could impact the fairness, inclusivity, and integrity of India's democratic process.
articles.imageDescription: The Parliament House of India, symbolizing legislative action.
articles.trivia: The Election Commission of India is a constitutional body responsible for conducting elections in India.

articles.headline: IIT Delhi Researchers Develop Novel Low-Cost Diagnostic Tool for Early Detection of Tropical Diseases
articles.explainer:
• Researchers at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Delhi have developed an innovative, low-cost diagnostic device for the early and rapid detection of prevalent tropical diseases.
• The portable device utilizes advanced biosensor technology to identify biomarkers of diseases like dengue, malaria, and chikungunya with high accuracy.
• Its affordability and ease of use make it particularly suitable for deployment in remote and resource-limited settings.
• This breakthrough has the potential to significantly improve public health outcomes by enabling timely diagnosis and treatment, thereby reducing disease spread.
• The development aligns with India's focus on indigenous innovation in healthcare technology and addressing neglected tropical diseases.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health.
prelimsPoints: Developed by: IIT Delhi researchers.
Purpose: low-cost, early, rapid detection of tropical diseases.
Technology used: advanced biosensor technology.
Target diseases: dengue, malaria, chikungunya.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of developing low-cost diagnostic tools for tropical diseases in a country like India, which has a high burden of such illnesses.
Analyze how indigenous innovations in biosensor technology can contribute to strengthening India's public health infrastructure, especially in rural areas.
Evaluate the potential impact of early and rapid detection on disease management, prevention of outbreaks, and reduction of healthcare costs.
Examine the role of academic institutions like IITs in fostering healthcare technology innovation and addressing national health challenges.
articles.imageDescription: A scientist in a lab coat holding a small diagnostic device.
articles.trivia: Tropical diseases are prevalent in tropical and subtropical regions and are often neglected, affecting millions of people, particularly in low-income countries.

articles.headline: BRICS Summit 2025 Concludes with Focus on De-dollarization and Enhanced Intra-bloc Trade Mechanisms
articles.explainer:
• The annual BRICS Summit for 2025 concluded with member nations reaffirming their commitment to strengthening multilateralism and reforming global governance.
• A major focus of the summit was the exploration of mechanisms for 'de-dollarization' in international trade and finance, including discussions on a common BRICS currency or alternative payment systems.
• Leaders emphasized boosting intra-bloc trade, investment, and cooperation in areas like technology transfer, sustainable development, and infrastructure projects.
• The summit also addressed global challenges such as climate change, food security, and regional conflicts, calling for peaceful resolutions.
• The expansion of BRICS membership and its growing influence on the global stage were key themes throughout the discussions.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
keyTerms: BRICS (Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa)
prelimsPoints: BRICS Summit 2025 focused on de-dollarization and intra-bloc trade.
Discussions included common BRICS currency or alternative payment systems.
Other focus areas: technology transfer, sustainable development, infrastructure.
BRICS members: Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa (and expanded members).
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving role of BRICS in reshaping the global economic and political order, particularly its focus on de-dollarization.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities for India within the BRICS framework, especially concerning intra-bloc trade and investment.
Evaluate the feasibility and implications of a common BRICS currency or alternative payment mechanisms for the international financial system.
Examine how BRICS cooperation can contribute to addressing global challenges such as climate change, food security, and sustainable development.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of BRICS nations displayed together at a summit venue.
articles.trivia: BRICS was originally formed in 2009, with South Africa joining in 2010. Its expansion in 2024 added new member countries.

articles.headline: Ministry of Culture Launches 'Virasat Darshan' Scheme to Promote Lesser-Known Heritage Sites
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture launched the 'Virasat Darshan' scheme, aimed at promoting and popularizing lesser-known heritage sites across India.
• The initiative seeks to diversify tourism beyond popular destinations, encourage local economies, and raise awareness about India's rich and varied cultural heritage.
• It involves developing infrastructure, guided tours, digital promotion, and community engagement programs around these sites.
• The scheme also emphasizes the conservation and sustainable management of these heritage assets, ensuring their preservation for future generations.
• 'Virasat Darshan' is expected to boost cultural tourism, create employment opportunities, and instill a sense of pride in local communities.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: Scheme name: 'Virasat Darshan'.
Launched by: Ministry of Culture.
Objective: promote lesser-known heritage sites.
Focus: infrastructure development, guided tours, digital promotion, community engagement.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of promoting lesser-known heritage sites for India's cultural tourism and local economic development.
Analyze the key features of the 'Virasat Darshan' scheme and its potential to diversify tourism offerings and reduce pressure on popular sites.
Evaluate the challenges in conserving and sustainably managing diverse heritage assets across India, particularly in remote areas.
Examine how cultural initiatives like this contribute to fostering national identity and pride in India's rich heritage.
articles.imageDescription: Ancient ruins of a lesser-known historical site, surrounded by greenery.
articles.trivia: India has numerous archaeological sites and historical monuments managed by the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) and state archaeology departments.

articles.headline: Coastal Security Exercise 'Sagar Kavach 2025' Successfully Conducted, Enhancing Maritime Domain Awareness
articles.explainer:
• The biennial coastal security exercise 'Sagar Kavach 2025' was successfully conducted along India's coastline, involving multiple security agencies.
• The two-day exercise simulated various maritime security threats, including infiltration attempts, terrorist attacks from the sea, and illegal fishing.
• Participating agencies included the Indian Navy, Indian Coast Guard, Marine Police, Customs, and other state and central agencies.
• The exercise aimed to validate standard operating procedures, enhance inter-agency coordination, and strengthen the overall coastal defense mechanism.
• 'Sagar Kavach' plays a crucial role in improving India's maritime domain awareness and preparedness against evolving threats.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: Exercise name: 'Sagar Kavach 2025'.
Type: Coastal security exercise.
Frequency: Biennial (conducted every two years).
Participants: Indian Navy, Indian Coast Guard, Marine Police, Customs, etc.
Objective: enhance inter-agency coordination, validate SOPs, strengthen coastal defense.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of maritime security threats to India's vast coastline and the importance of exercises like 'Sagar Kavach'.
Analyze the role of various agencies (Indian Navy, Coast Guard, Marine Police) in ensuring comprehensive coastal defense and maritime domain awareness.
Evaluate the challenges in achieving seamless inter-agency coordination for coastal security and suggest measures for improvement.
Examine how such exercises contribute to India's overall national security preparedness against non-state actors and other maritime challenges.
articles.imageDescription: Indian Coast Guard ships patrolling the coastline during a maritime exercise.
articles.trivia: India has a coastline of over 7,500 km, including island territories, making coastal security a complex and critical challenge.

articles.headline: New Species of Orchid Discovered in Western Ghats, Highlighting Biodiversity Hotspot Status
articles.explainer:
• Botanists conducting a survey in the biodiversity-rich Western Ghats have identified and documented a previously unknown species of orchid.
• The discovery underscores the immense biodiversity of the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the world's eight 'hottest' biodiversity hotspots.
• The new orchid species, yet to be formally named, exhibits unique morphological characteristics, distinguishing it from known varieties.
• This finding emphasizes the urgent need for continued conservation efforts to protect the fragile ecosystems of the region from habitat loss and climate change.
• Such discoveries contribute significantly to global botanical knowledge and highlight the importance of systematic ecological surveys.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and Climate Change.
prelimsPoints: New orchid species discovered in Western Ghats.
Western Ghats is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and biodiversity hotspot.
Discovery highlights the need for conservation.
Orchids are a diverse family of flowering plants.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the threats it faces.
Analyze how the discovery of new species contributes to our understanding of biodiversity and the urgency of conservation efforts.
Evaluate the various conservation strategies employed in the Western Ghats and suggest measures for their enhancement.
Examine the impact of climate change and habitat fragmentation on endemic species in vulnerable ecosystems like the Western Ghats.
articles.imageDescription: A close-up photograph of a vibrant, newly discovered orchid flower.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are home to at least 325 globally threatened species, including plants, mammals, birds, amphibians, reptiles, and fish.

articles.headline: India and Sri Lanka Launch Joint Scientific Research Program in Oceanography and Marine Biology
articles.explainer:
• India and Sri Lanka have initiated a joint scientific research program focused on oceanography, marine biology, and sustainable fishing practices.
• The collaboration aims to enhance understanding of the Indian Ocean's ecosystem, address challenges like marine pollution, and promote blue economy initiatives.
• Researchers from both countries will conduct joint expeditions, share data, and develop sustainable management strategies for shared marine resources.
• This partnership underscores growing bilateral cooperation in science and technology, crucial for regional maritime security and environmental protection.
• The program is expected to foster knowledge exchange and build capacity in marine sciences for both nations.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Joint research program between India and Sri Lanka.
Focus: oceanography, marine biology, sustainable fishing.
Objectives: understand Indian Ocean ecosystem, combat marine pollution, promote blue economy.
Involves joint expeditions and data sharing.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the Indian Ocean Region for India and Sri Lanka and the need for bilateral scientific cooperation.
Analyze the scope and benefits of a joint research program in oceanography and marine biology for both nations, particularly in addressing marine pollution.
Evaluate how this collaboration can contribute to the development of a sustainable 'blue economy' and enhance regional maritime security.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in fostering scientific and technological cooperation between India and its maritime neighbors.
articles.imageDescription: A research vessel sailing in the Indian Ocean with the flags of India and Sri Lanka.
articles.trivia: The Indian Ocean is the third-largest of the world's oceanic divisions and is strategically vital for global trade routes.

highlights: India launches 'National AI & Data Governance Framework 2025' to ensure ethical AI deployment and data privacy, a major step in digital transformation.
Supreme Court upholds 'Right to Disconnect' legislation, emphasizing employee well-being and privacy, setting a new precedent for labor laws.
ISRO successfully launches 'Shukrayaan-2' mission to Venus, bolstering India's deep-space exploration capabilities and planetary science research.
RBI maintains repo rate at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive time, signaling continued vigilance on inflation amidst global economic uncertainties.
BRICS Summit 2025 concludes with a strong focus on de-dollarization strategies and enhancing intra-bloc trade and economic cooperation.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'National AI & Data Governance Framework 2025', consider the following statements:
1. The framework aims to establish an 'AI Ethics Council' and a 'National Data Protection Authority'.
2. It primarily focuses on encouraging global AI companies to set up R&D centers in India without any regulatory oversight.
3. The policy is a part of India's broader vision for Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The framework aims to establish an 'AI Ethics Council' and a 'National Data Protection Authority' to guide AI development and data management. Statement 2 is incorrect: The framework emphasizes ethical AI principles, data privacy, and responsible use of AI, implying regulatory oversight, not a free-for-all for global companies. Statement 3 is correct: The policy aligns with India's digital public infrastructure (DPI) vision for inclusive digital transformation.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following joint military exercises is conducted between India and Nepal?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The joint military exercise 'Surya Kiran' is conducted between India and Nepal. 'Maitree' is between India and Thailand, 'Sampriti' is between India and Bangladesh, and 'Mitra Shakti' is between India and Sri Lanka.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 'Electoral Reforms Bill 2025' recently passed by Lok Sabha:
1. The Bill proposes linking voter registration with Aadhaar.
2. It aims to reduce the powers of the Election Commission of India (ECI) in regulating campaign finance.
3. The Bill focuses on streamlining voter registration and curbing electoral malpractices.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Bill introduces changes aimed at streamlining voter registration by linking it with Aadhaar. Statement 2 is incorrect: The Bill proposes *enhanced* powers for the Election Commission of India (ECI) to enforce the Model Code of Conduct and regulate anonymous donations. Statement 3 is correct: The Bill's primary objectives are to streamline voter registration and curb electoral malpractices.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'National Wetlands Conservation and Management Program 2025' launched by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change is primarily aimed at:
options: (a) Promoting commercial aquaculture in all identified wetlands.
(b) Holistic protection, sustainable use, and restoration of wetland ecosystems.
(c) Converting wetlands into agricultural lands to enhance food security.
(d) Developing wetlands exclusively for ecotourism without local community involvement.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Holistic protection, sustainable use, and restoration of wetland ecosystems.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'National Wetlands Conservation and Management Program 2025' aims for holistic protection, sustainable use, and restoration of India's diverse wetland ecosystems, integrating community participation and scientific research. Options (a), (c), and (d) contradict the principles of conservation and sustainable management of wetlands.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a primary focus of India's 'Shukrayaan-2' mission?
options: (a) Studying the Venusian atmosphere and surface composition.
(b) Investigating the geological and atmospheric evolution of Venus.
(c) Searching for signs of microbial life in Venus's upper atmosphere.
(d) Enhancing India's capabilities in deep-space exploration.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Searching for signs of microbial life in Venus's upper atmosphere.
prelimsMocks.explanation: While 'Shukrayaan-2' aims to study the Venusian atmosphere, surface, and geological/atmospheric evolution (a and b), and enhance India's deep-space capabilities (d), its primary objectives as described do not explicitly include searching for microbial life. Such a search would typically require specific bio-signature detection payloads, which are not mentioned as a core objective for this orbiter mission.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the 'National AI & Data Governance Framework 2025' and its potential to balance innovation with ethical considerations and data privacy in India's digital transformation journey.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation; E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the framework and its objectives. Discuss its strengths in promoting ethical AI, data privacy, and leveraging DPI. Analyze potential challenges such as implementation complexities, data security, and balancing innovation with regulation. Conclude with recommendations for effective governance.

mainsMocks.question: Examine the strategic and economic significance of the ongoing India-EU Free Trade Agreement negotiations. Discuss the key challenges and opportunities for India in securing a comprehensive and mutually beneficial agreement.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests; Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by highlighting India-EU trade relations. Detail the strategic importance (market access, investment) and economic benefits (boost to exports, FDI). Discuss challenges like IPR, market access for sensitive sectors, and non-tariff barriers. Conclude with how India can leverage its position for a favorable outcome.

mainsMocks.question: Despite various initiatives, air pollution remains a persistent public health challenge in major Indian cities. Discuss the multifaceted causes of urban air pollution and critically evaluate the effectiveness of the National Clean Air Program (NCAP) in addressing this crisis. Suggest further measures for a holistic approach.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Start by stating the severity of air pollution. Elaborate on causes like vehicular emissions, industrial pollution, construction, stubble burning, and geographical factors. Critically assess NCAP's achievements and limitations. Propose solutions including technological interventions, inter-state cooperation, public awareness, and strengthening regulatory frameworks.

quickBytes.text: India's 'National AI & Data Governance Framework 2025' aims for ethical AI and data privacy.
R. V. Devraj, former Karnataka MLA and KSRTC Chairman, passed away at 67.
RBI maintained the repo rate at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
India-EU FTA negotiations are in their crucial final phase, focusing on market access and IPR.
The 'Right to Disconnect' legislation was upheld by the Supreme Court.
ISRO successfully launched 'Shukrayaan-2' mission to Venus.
The 'Surya Kiran XV' joint military exercise between India and Nepal concluded.
A new PLI scheme for advanced semiconductor manufacturing was announced.
National Health Mission 2.0 emphasizes preventive healthcare and digital health records.
BRICS Summit 2025 focused on de-dollarization and enhanced intra-bloc trade.

GS1.headline: Ministry of Culture Launches 'Virasat Darshan' Scheme to Promote Lesser-Known Heritage Sites
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture launched the 'Virasat Darshan' scheme, aimed at promoting and popularizing lesser-known heritage sites across India.
• The initiative seeks to diversify tourism beyond popular destinations, encourage local economies, and raise awareness about India's rich and varied cultural heritage.
• It involves developing infrastructure, guided tours, digital promotion, and community engagement programs around these sites.
• The scheme also emphasizes the conservation and sustainable management of these heritage assets, ensuring their preservation for future generations.
• 'Virasat Darshan' is expected to boost cultural tourism, create employment opportunities, and instill a sense of pride in local communities.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: Scheme name: 'Virasat Darshan'.
Launched by: Ministry of Culture.
Objective: promote lesser-known heritage sites.
Focus: infrastructure development, guided tours, digital promotion, community engagement.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of promoting lesser-known heritage sites for India's cultural tourism and local economic development.
Analyze the key features of the 'Virasat Darshan' scheme and its potential to diversify tourism offerings and reduce pressure on popular sites.
Evaluate the challenges in conserving and sustainably managing diverse heritage assets across India, particularly in remote areas.
Examine how cultural initiatives like this contribute to fostering national identity and pride in India's rich heritage.
GS1.imageDescription: Ancient ruins of a lesser-known historical site, surrounded by greenery.
GS1.trivia: India has numerous archaeological sites and historical monuments managed by the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) and state archaeology departments.

GS2.headline: India Unveils 'National AI & Data Governance Framework 2025' to Drive Inclusive Digital Transformation
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved a comprehensive framework to guide the development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and manage data governance across sectors.
• The framework emphasizes ethical AI principles, data privacy, security, and the responsible use of AI for public good, aligning with India's digital public infrastructure (DPI) vision.
• Key components include establishing an 'AI Ethics Council', a 'National Data Protection Authority', and guidelines for anonymization and secure data sharing.
• The policy aims to foster innovation while mitigating risks associated with AI, ensuring equitable access and benefits for all citizens.
• It is seen as a crucial step towards making India a global leader in responsible AI and digital economy.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential
Role of IT in governance
prelimsPoints: The framework is named 'National AI & Data Governance Framework 2025'.
It was approved by the Union Cabinet.
Key bodies to be established: AI Ethics Council, National Data Protection Authority.
Focus areas: ethical AI, data privacy, security, responsible AI use.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of a national AI and data governance framework for India's digital future.
Analyze the challenges in implementing ethical AI and data privacy regulations in a diverse country like India.
Evaluate how the framework can leverage India's Digital Public Infrastructure for inclusive growth and innovation.
Examine the role of the proposed AI Ethics Council and National Data Protection Authority in ensuring responsible AI development.
GS2.imageDescription: Illustration depicting digital infrastructure and AI symbols converging over a map of India.
GS2.trivia: India's digital public infrastructure, including UPI and Aadhaar, is often cited as a global model for inclusive digital transformation.

GS2.headline: Veteran Karnataka Politician R. V. Devraj Passes Away at 67
GS2.explainer:
• R. V. Devraj, a prominent figure in Karnataka politics and former MLA, passed away at the age of 67.
• He served as a Member of the Legislative Assembly (MLA) for Karnataka from 2004 to 2008.
• Devraj also held the distinguished position of Chairman of the Karnataka State Road Transport Corporation (KSRTC) from 2000 to 2007.
• He was known for his contributions to public service and transport sector reforms in the state.
• His demise marks the loss of an experienced leader in state politics.
gsSubTopics: Important personalities
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
prelimsPoints: R. V. Devraj was a former MLA from Karnataka (2004-2008).
He served as Chairman of KSRTC (2000-2007).
His age at passing was 67.
GS2.imageDescription: Black and white portrait of R. V. Devraj.
GS2.trivia: State Road Transport Corporations (SRTCs) play a vital role in providing affordable public transport, especially in rural areas, and are often headed by political appointees.

GS2.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Enter Crucial Final Phase, Focus on Market Access and IPR
GS2.explainer:
• Negotiations for the ambitious India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have entered their final and most crucial phase.
• Key sticking points include greater market access for agricultural products, automotive, and services, alongside robust provisions for Intellectual Property Rights (IPR) protection.
• Both sides express optimism for a breakthrough by mid-2026, aiming to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment.
• The FTA is expected to provide Indian industries with preferential access to the large EU market and attract European investment into India.
• Successful conclusion would mark a strategic geopolitical and economic partnership between two of the world's largest economies.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India-EU FTA negotiations are in their final phase.
Key areas of discussion: market access (agriculture, automotive, services) and IPR.
Expected completion by mid-2026.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic and economic significance of a comprehensive Free Trade Agreement between India and the European Union.
Analyze the major challenges and opportunities for India in negotiating market access and IPR provisions with the EU.
Evaluate the potential impact of the India-EU FTA on India's domestic industries and overall economic growth.
Examine how this FTA could reshape global trade dynamics and India's position in multilateral trade forums.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union displayed together.
GS2.trivia: The EU is India's third-largest trading partner, and India is the EU's 10th largest trading partner.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds 'Right to Disconnect' Legislation, Citing Employee Well-being and Privacy
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India delivered a landmark judgment, upholding the constitutional validity of the 'Right to Disconnect' legislation.
• The law, enacted by several states, grants employees the right to not respond to work-related communications outside of designated working hours.
• The Court emphasized the importance of work-life balance, mental health, and the right to privacy for employees in the digital age.
• It clarified that while employers have a right to reasonable expectation of availability, it cannot infringe upon an individual's personal time and mental well-being.
• This ruling is expected to influence national labor laws and corporate policies, promoting healthier workplace cultures.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: The Supreme Court upheld the 'Right to Disconnect' legislation.
It cited employee well-being and privacy as key reasons.
The legislation allows employees to ignore work communications outside work hours.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional basis for the 'Right to Disconnect' in India, linking it to fundamental rights like the right to life and privacy.
Analyze the socio-economic implications of this judgment for both employees and employers in the Indian context.
Examine how the 'Right to Disconnect' contributes to employee mental health and work-life balance in the increasingly digitalized workplace.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in implementing such a right across diverse sectors and industries in India.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel resting on law books, symbolizing a court judgment.
GS2.trivia: France was one of the first countries to introduce a 'right to disconnect' law in 2017.

GS2.headline: National Health Mission 2.0 Rolled Out, Prioritizing Preventive Healthcare and Digital Health Records
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare launched 'National Health Mission 2.0' (NHM 2.0), a revamped version of the flagship health program.
• NHM 2.0 places strong emphasis on preventive and promotive healthcare, alongside strengthening primary and secondary health infrastructure.
• A key feature is the nationwide implementation of digital health records and telehealth services under the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM).
• The mission aims to address health disparities, improve maternal and child health outcomes, and combat non-communicable diseases (NCDs) more effectively.
• It seeks to achieve universal access to quality healthcare services, aligning with the vision of 'Health for All'.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
GS2.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: Program name: 'National Health Mission 2.0'.
Launched by: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare.
Key focus areas: preventive healthcare, digital health records, strengthening primary/secondary health.
Integrates with: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM).
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of India's public health policy from NHM to NHM 2.0, highlighting the shifts in priorities and strategies.
Analyze the potential impact of digital health records and telehealth services under NHM 2.0 on healthcare accessibility and efficiency in rural areas.
Evaluate the challenges in achieving universal health coverage and addressing health disparities through the new mission.
Examine the importance of preventive and promotive healthcare in reducing the burden of non-communicable diseases in India.
GS2.imageDescription: A doctor interacting with a patient using a tablet, symbolizing digital healthcare.
GS2.trivia: The original National Health Mission (NHM) was launched in 2013 by subsuming the National Rural Health Mission (NRHM) and the National Urban Health Mission (NUHM).

GS2.headline: India Reiterates Call for Comprehensive UNSC Reforms at UN General Assembly Session
GS2.explainer:
• India's Permanent Representative to the UN strongly advocated for comprehensive reforms of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) during the ongoing UN General Assembly session.
• India emphasized the need for an expanded and more representative UNSC that reflects contemporary global realities, including increasing the number of permanent and non-permanent members.
• The call highlighted the inadequacy of the current structure, which dates back to 1945, in addressing 21st-century challenges effectively.
• India, along with other G4 nations (Brazil, Germany, Japan), has consistently pushed for a permanent seat on the Security Council.
• Reforms are deemed crucial for enhancing the legitimacy, effectiveness, and credibility of the UN's principal organ responsible for international peace and security.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India advocates for UNSC reforms.
Key demand: expansion of permanent and non-permanent members.
India is part of the G4 nations (Brazil, Germany, Japan, India) pushing for permanent seats.
UNSC structure dates back to 1945.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's long-standing demand for a permanent seat in the UNSC and the justifications for its inclusion.
Analyze the arguments for and against the expansion of the UNSC, considering the interests of various member states.
Evaluate the implications of the current UNSC structure on its effectiveness and legitimacy in addressing global conflicts and crises.
Examine the role of the G4 nations in advocating for UNSC reforms and the challenges they face in achieving consensus.
GS2.imageDescription: The United Nations General Assembly hall in New York.
GS2.trivia: The UN Security Council has 15 members: five permanent members with veto power (China, France, Russia, United Kingdom, United States) and ten non-permanent members elected for two-year terms.

GS2.headline: Lok Sabha Passes 'Electoral Reforms Bill 2025', Aiming to Streamline Voter Registration and Campaign Finance
GS2.explainer:
• The Lok Sabha passed the 'Electoral Reforms Bill 2025' after extensive debate, which now moves to the Rajya Sabha for consideration.
• The Bill introduces significant changes aimed at streamlining voter registration by linking it with Aadhaar, ensuring transparent campaign finance, and curbing electoral malpractices.
• Provisions include enhanced powers for the Election Commission of India (ECI) to enforce the Model Code of Conduct and regulate anonymous donations.
• Opposition parties raised concerns regarding potential privacy infringements with Aadhaar linkage and the impact on electoral transparency.
• The government asserts the Bill will strengthen democratic processes and make elections more fair and inclusive.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: Bill name: 'Electoral Reforms Bill 2025'.
Passed by: Lok Sabha, moves to Rajya Sabha.
Key features: Aadhaar linkage for voter registration, transparent campaign finance, enhanced ECI powers.
Concerns raised: privacy, electoral transparency.
mainsPoints: Discuss the need for electoral reforms in India, particularly concerning voter registration and campaign finance.
Analyze the proposed changes in the 'Electoral Reforms Bill 2025', focusing on the implications of Aadhaar linkage and ECI's enhanced powers.
Evaluate the arguments for and against the Bill, particularly concerning privacy concerns and the balance between transparency and individual rights.
Examine how these reforms could impact the fairness, inclusivity, and integrity of India's democratic process.
GS2.imageDescription: The Parliament House of India, symbolizing legislative action.
GS2.trivia: The Election Commission of India is a constitutional body responsible for conducting elections in India.

GS2.headline: BRICS Summit 2025 Concludes with Focus on De-dollarization and Enhanced Intra-bloc Trade Mechanisms
GS2.explainer:
• The annual BRICS Summit for 2025 concluded with member nations reaffirming their commitment to strengthening multilateralism and reforming global governance.
• A major focus of the summit was the exploration of mechanisms for 'de-dollarization' in international trade and finance, including discussions on a common BRICS currency or alternative payment systems.
• Leaders emphasized boosting intra-bloc trade, investment, and cooperation in areas like technology transfer, sustainable development, and infrastructure projects.
• The summit also addressed global challenges such as climate change, food security, and regional conflicts, calling for peaceful resolutions.
• The expansion of BRICS membership and its growing influence on the global stage were key themes throughout the discussions.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
keyTerms: BRICS (Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa)
prelimsPoints: BRICS Summit 2025 focused on de-dollarization and intra-bloc trade.
Discussions included common BRICS currency or alternative payment systems.
Other focus areas: technology transfer, sustainable development, infrastructure.
BRICS members: Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa (and expanded members).
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving role of BRICS in reshaping the global economic and political order, particularly its focus on de-dollarization.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities for India within the BRICS framework, especially concerning intra-bloc trade and investment.
Evaluate the feasibility and implications of a common BRICS currency or alternative payment mechanisms for the international financial system.
Examine how BRICS cooperation can contribute to addressing global challenges such as climate change, food security, and sustainable development.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of BRICS nations displayed together at a summit venue.
GS2.trivia: BRICS was originally formed in 2009, with South Africa joining in 2010. Its expansion in 2024 added new member countries.

GS2.headline: India and Sri Lanka Launch Joint Scientific Research Program in Oceanography and Marine Biology
GS2.explainer:
• India and Sri Lanka have initiated a joint scientific research program focused on oceanography, marine biology, and sustainable fishing practices.
• The collaboration aims to enhance understanding of the Indian Ocean's ecosystem, address challenges like marine pollution, and promote blue economy initiatives.
• Researchers from both countries will conduct joint expeditions, share data, and develop sustainable management strategies for shared marine resources.
• This partnership underscores growing bilateral cooperation in science and technology, crucial for regional maritime security and environmental protection.
• The program is expected to foster knowledge exchange and build capacity in marine sciences for both nations.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Joint research program between India and Sri Lanka.
Focus: oceanography, marine biology, sustainable fishing.
Objectives: understand Indian Ocean ecosystem, combat marine pollution, promote blue economy.
Involves joint expeditions and data sharing.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the Indian Ocean Region for India and Sri Lanka and the need for bilateral scientific cooperation.
Analyze the scope and benefits of a joint research program in oceanography and marine biology for both nations, particularly in addressing marine pollution.
Evaluate how this collaboration can contribute to the development of a sustainable 'blue economy' and enhance regional maritime security.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in fostering scientific and technological cooperation between India and its maritime neighbors.
GS2.imageDescription: A research vessel sailing in the Indian Ocean with the flags of India and Sri Lanka.
GS2.trivia: The Indian Ocean is the third-largest of the world's oceanic divisions and is strategically vital for global trade routes.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% Amidst Global Headwinds, Focus on Inflation Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach amidst persistent global economic uncertainties and domestic inflationary pressures, particularly from food prices.
• RBI Governor highlighted the need for continued vigilance to bring inflation within the target band of 2-6% sustainably.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure that inflation progressively aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts suggest this stance indicates a preference for stability and a wait-and-watch approach before considering any rate cuts.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Current repo rate: 6.5%.
This is the sixth consecutive time the rate has been unchanged.
RBI's inflation target band: 2-6%.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is responsible for setting the repo rate.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate in the current economic scenario.
Discuss the impact of sustained high-interest rates on India's economic growth and investment climate.
Examine the challenges faced by the RBI in managing inflation, particularly the role of supply-side factors like food prices.
Evaluate the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance and its implications for future monetary policy decisions.
GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: New 'National Wetlands Conservation and Management Program 2025' Launched for Holistic Protection
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched an ambitious 'National Wetlands Conservation and Management Program 2025'.
• The program aims for holistic protection, sustainable use, and restoration of India's diverse wetland ecosystems.
• It integrates community participation, scientific research, and capacity building for effective wetland management at local levels.
• Emphasis is placed on inventorying and mapping all wetlands, categorizing them based on ecological significance, and developing site-specific management plans.
• This initiative underscores India's commitment to Ramsar Convention principles and biodiversity conservation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and Climate Change.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Program name: 'National Wetlands Conservation and Management Program 2025'.
Launched by: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
Key aspects: holistic protection, sustainable use, community participation, scientific research, capacity building.
Aligned with: Ramsar Convention.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and economic significance of wetlands in India and the threats they face.
Analyze the key features of the 'National Wetlands Conservation and Management Program 2025' and its potential effectiveness.
Evaluate the role of community participation and scientific research in the successful implementation of wetland conservation initiatives.
Examine how this program aligns with India's international commitments under the Ramsar Convention and broader biodiversity goals.
GS3.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with migratory birds and lush vegetation.
GS3.trivia: India has over 75 Ramsar Sites, covering a large ecological area, designated as Wetlands of International Importance.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches 'Shukrayaan-2' Mission to Venus, Expanding India's Interplanetary Footprint
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved another milestone with the successful launch of 'Shukrayaan-2', India's second mission to Venus.
• The mission aims to study the Venusian atmosphere, surface, and subsurface composition, focusing on its geological and atmospheric evolution.
• Shukrayaan-2 carries a suite of advanced scientific payloads developed by Indian and international research institutions.
• This mission reinforces India's growing capabilities in deep-space exploration and planetary science.
• Data from Shukrayaan-2 is expected to provide crucial insights into Venus's extreme environment and potential for past habitability.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: Mission name: 'Shukrayaan-2'.
Target planet: Venus.
Launching agency: ISRO.
Primary objective: study Venusian atmosphere, surface, and subsurface.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's 'Shukrayaan-2' mission in advancing planetary science and space exploration.
Analyze the scientific objectives of studying Venus and how they contribute to our understanding of planetary evolution.
Evaluate the role of international collaboration in ISRO's deep-space missions and its implications for global scientific cooperation.
Examine the challenges involved in designing and executing missions to planets with extreme environments like Venus.
GS3.imageDescription: Artist's impression of the Shukrayaan-2 orbiter approaching Venus.
GS3.trivia: Venus is often called Earth's 'sister planet' due to its similar size and mass, but its extremely hot and dense atmosphere makes it vastly different.

GS3.headline: India-Nepal Joint Military Exercise 'Surya Kiran XV' Concludes, Enhancing Bilateral Security Cooperation
GS3.explainer:
• The 15th edition of the joint military exercise 'Surya Kiran' between India and Nepal concluded successfully in Uttarakhand.
• The exercise focused on enhancing interoperability in counter-terrorism operations, disaster relief, and humanitarian assistance in mountainous terrain.
• Troops from both armies shared best practices and tactical skills through simulated scenarios and joint drills.
• 'Surya Kiran' is a significant annual event that strengthens defense cooperation and fosters mutual understanding between the two nations.
• The exercise underscored the deep-rooted strategic partnership and friendly ties between India and Nepal.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: Exercise name: 'Surya Kiran XV'.
Participating countries: India and Nepal.
Focus: counter-terrorism, disaster relief, humanitarian assistance in mountainous terrain.
Location for this edition: Uttarakhand.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of joint military exercises like 'Surya Kiran' for India's border security and regional stability.
Analyze how such exercises contribute to enhancing interoperability and mutual trust between the armed forces of India and Nepal.
Evaluate the evolving nature of India-Nepal defense cooperation in the context of changing geopolitical dynamics in the Himalayan region.
Examine the role of military diplomacy in strengthening bilateral relations between neighboring countries.
GS3.imageDescription: Indian and Nepali soldiers conducting a joint drill in mountainous terrain.
GS3.trivia: The 'Surya Kiran' exercise is held alternately in India and Nepal.

GS3.headline: Government Announces New PLI Scheme for Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing, Boosting 'Make in India'
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government unveiled a new Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme specifically tailored for advanced semiconductor manufacturing and design.
• This scheme aims to attract global semiconductor majors to set up fabrication units and design centers in India.
• It offers substantial financial incentives, tax breaks, and infrastructure support to foster a robust domestic semiconductor ecosystem.
• The initiative is critical for India's strategic autonomy in electronics manufacturing and reducing dependence on imports.
• It aligns with the 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' visions, positioning India as a global hub for semiconductor production.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
GS3.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsPoints: New PLI scheme targets advanced semiconductor manufacturing and design.
Objective: attract global majors, foster domestic ecosystem.
Part of 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives.
Offers financial incentives, tax breaks, infrastructure support.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing a robust domestic semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem for India's economic and national security.
Analyze the key features of the new PLI scheme for semiconductors and its potential to attract foreign direct investment (FDI) and boost local production.
Evaluate the challenges India faces in becoming a global hub for semiconductor manufacturing, including infrastructure, skilled labor, and technological expertise.
Examine how this initiative aligns with India's broader goals of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' and digital sovereignty.
GS3.imageDescription: A microchip with interconnected circuits, symbolizing semiconductor technology.
GS3.trivia: The global semiconductor industry is highly concentrated, with Taiwan and South Korea dominating advanced chip manufacturing.

GS3.headline: New Report Highlights Severe Air Pollution Levels in Major Indian Cities, Calls for Stricter NCAP Implementation
GS3.explainer:
• A recent report by a leading environmental watchdog revealed alarming levels of air pollution in several major Indian cities, consistently exceeding safe limits.
• The report attributed the pollution to a combination of vehicular emissions, industrial activities, construction dust, and stubble burning in neighboring regions.
• It urged for the stringent and expedited implementation of the National Clean Air Program (NCAP) and greater inter-state cooperation.
• The findings underscore severe public health implications, including respiratory diseases and reduced life expectancy.
• Experts called for a multi-sectoral approach involving policy changes, technological solutions, and public awareness campaigns to tackle the crisis.
